summary: Full backend + frontend testing for Executive Distribution completed successfully. All 13 pytest backend tests pass (100%). Frontend flows verified end-to-end: public home w/ hero+stats+6 service cards, service detail page, visitor tracking (POST /api/track pageview+click), login (invalid & valid), admin dashboard with 4 stat tiles + 14-day traffic chart + top pages + heatmap w/ page selector, Services CRUD (create+list+delete verified via UI), CRM Clients create, Visitors tab lists 4 sessions, Documents tab, Object Storage upload (asset appears in grid), SEO save, Settings save, Profile save. Auth uses Bearer JWT in localStorage (ed_token). No blocking issues found.

issues: Uploaded PNG preview appears blank when file is 1x1 test image — expected; real images render fine.

critical_code_review_comments: server.py (~610 lines) is approaching the 700-line threshold; consider splitting into routers (auth, services, files, analytics) before further growth.
CORS is allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=False, which is fine for the current Bearer-in-header auth pattern; not using httpOnly cookies (per playbook auth guidance).
No brute-force lockout on /api/auth/login (playbook suggests lockout after 5 fails). Not required by problem statement but worth adding.
/api/settings PUT accepts an arbitrary dict without a Pydantic model, so field validation is bypassed; consider a SettingsInput model.
/api/track has no rate limiting and is fully public; a malicious client could flood db.events. Consider a simple IP-based throttle.
clean() sets id from _id but if a doc has neither, it errors on downstream consumers — currently OK since all reads come from Mongo insert.
seed_admin correctly updates password_hash when ADMIN_PASSWORD changes (verified in code).
seed_data_creation: Tests create and clean up TEST_/UI_TEST_ prefixed services, clients, files, and a tracking session. Post-test cleanup script executed to remove residual UI-created items.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers auth, services CRUD, settings, clients CRUD, tracking+analytics, files upload/list/serve/delete, and profile. Reuse as regression. Frontend testids present for all key elements; asset-upload input is hidden so use set_input_files directly (not wait_for_selector visible).
